race.comment: BETTOR BEACH (9) is a lightly raced but exciting filly who spanked her rivals by nearly 30m when resuming in Victoria. That was her first outing for more than six months so it is sure to have improved her. Her only other two outings produced a debut win over Group 1 winner Arrhythmia and a fourth to that filly in the Breeders Crown Final. Draws on the back row but she is untapped. SPARKLING SEA (5) had a tough draw on the back row and worked last week so it may pay to be forgiving. Has proven herself to be one NSW's best fillies so from a better draw she is worth another chance. SWIFT CODE (4) was solid from the back row last week and was racing well prior. Decent draw so include in exotics along with SEASIDE BOULEVARD (8) who draws for a trail and deserves respect off her tough Redcliffe Oaks win two back.
